Oxidative burst: NADPH oxidase → superoxide (O₂⁻•) → H₂O₂ → HOCl (myeloperoxidase) — extremely bactericidal. iNOS → nitric oxide (NO•) → peroxynitrite (ONOO⁻). (5) Antigen presentation: bacterial protein fragments (peptides, 13-25 amino acids) loaded onto MHC class II molecules (assembled in ER, processed in Golgi) → transported to cell surface → presented to CD4⁺ helper T cells → adaptive immune response initiation. ER and Golgi are essential for MHC synthesis and processing. (6) Waste disposal: indigested remnants expelled by exocytosis. (7) Summary of organelles: cell membrane (recognition/engulfment), cytoskeleton-actin (pseudopodia), lysosomes (digestion), mitochondria (ATP), ER (MHC/protein synthesis), Golgi (MHC processing/lysosomal enzyme delivery), endosomes (maturation pathway).

quiz_questions.question_text: A cell biologist discovers that a rare genetic mutation causes a patient's cells to completely lack peroxisomes (Zellweger spectrum disorder). The patient, an infant, has severe neurological dysfunction, liver disease, and abnormal facial features. Why would the absence of this single, seemingly minor organelle cause such devastating multi-organ failure?

options.text: Peroxisomes are the organelles that produce all steroid hormones — without them, the infant has no cortisol, estrogen, or testosterone, causing hormonal collapse, involving the coordinated activity of organelles, enzymes, and signaling pathways that regulate cellular function and reproduction

Peroxisomes are needed only for detoxifying alcohol — the infant's disease is actually caused by accidental alcohol exposure, not a genetic defect in peroxisomes, as governed by the molecular machinery of gene expression, protein synthesis, and intracellular transport within living cells overall

Peroxisomes are the primary site of protein synthesis — without them, cells cannot make any proteins and all organ systems fail simultaneously, through the action of specific biochemical pathways and regulatory mechanisms that maintain cellular homeostasis and normal growth

Peroxisomes perform several essential metabolic functions that no other organelle can fully compensate for: (1) very-long-chain fatty acid (VLCFA) β-oxidation, (2) synthesis of plasmalogens, (3) bile acid synthesis, (4) hydrogen peroxide metabolism — without peroxisomes, VLCFAs accumulate, plasmalogens are absent, bile synthesis fails (liver disease), and H₂O₂ accumulates (oxidative damage)

quiz_questions.explanation: Zellweger spectrum disorder — peroxisomal absence: (1) Peroxisomes: single-membrane organelles (~0.1-1 μm). Named for hydrogen peroxide (H₂O₂) — many peroxisomal oxidases produce H₂O₂ as byproduct → catalase breaks it down. ~50 enzymes. (2) Essential functions: VLCFA β-oxidation (fatty acids >C22 can ONLY be shortened in peroxisomes; after shortening to ~C8, acetyl-CoA units transferred to mitochondria for further oxidation). Plasmalogen synthesis (ether-phospholipids — ~18% of brain phospholipids, critical component of myelin — the insulating sheath around axons). α-oxidation of branched-chain fatty acids (phytanic acid from dairy/meat). Bile acid synthesis (side-chain oxidation of cholesterol). Amino acid metabolism (D-amino acid oxidase, L-pipecolic acid oxidase). H₂O₂ detoxification (catalase — one of the fastest enzymes known: ~40 million reactions/sec). (3) Zellweger syndrome (most severe of Zellweger spectrum): caused by mutations in PEX genes (peroxins — ~16 known) needed for peroxisome biogenesis/assembly. Most common: PEX1 mutations (~60% of cases). Autosomal recessive. ~1/50,000 births. (4) Pathology: VLCFA accumulation (C26:0 elevated in blood — diagnostic biomarker) → disrupts cell membranes, especially myelin → demyelination, neuronal migration defects → severe intellectual disability, seizures, hypotonia. Plasmalogen deficiency → defective myelin formation → compounds neurological damage. Bile acid synthetic intermediates accumulate → liver dysfunction (hepatomegaly, jaundice, fibrosis). Brain MRI: polymicrogyria (abnormal cortical gyral pattern), demyelination, germinolytic cysts. (5) Prognosis: most severe form (Zellweger syndrome) → death within 1st year. Milder forms (neonatal adrenoleukodystrophy, infantile Refsum disease): variable survival. No cure; treatment is supportive. (6) Demonstrates: 'minor' organelles perform unique, irreplaceable metabolic functions. The cell is an integrated system — removing any component impacts the whole.
